phrases.intent_en: Ask to join a distillery tour
phrases.text_local: Bonjour, je voudrais participer à la visite de la distillerie.
phrases.pronunciation: bohn-ZHOOR, zhuh voo-DRAY par-tee-see-PAY ah lah vee-ZEET duh lah dees-tee-luh-REE.
phrases.cultural_note: France has its own great distilling traditions — Cognac, Armagnac, Calvados. A French speaker will feel right at home talking about distillation ('la distillation') and aging ('le vieillissement').

responses.french: Bien sûr ! La prochaine visite commence dans quinze minutes. Ça fait quinze dollars.
responses.english: Of course! The next tour starts in fifteen minutes. That's fifteen dollars.
responses.phonetic: byan SOOR ! lah pro-SHEN vee-ZEET koh-MAHNS dahn KANZ mee-NOOT. sah fay KANZ dol-AR.

responses.french: Je vais vous inscrire. Votre prénom ?
responses.english: I'll sign you up. Your first name?
responses.phonetic: zhuh vay vooz an-SKREER. votr pray-NOHN ?

phrases.text_local: Comment est fabriqué le bourbon ? Quelle est la différence avec le whisky ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN ay fah-bree-KAY luh BUR-bohn ? kel ay lah dee-fay-RAHNS ah-VEK luh WEES-kee ?
phrases.cultural_note: Bourbon must by law be made from at least 51% corn and aged in new charred oak barrels — unlike Scotch whisky. This is a great way to show curiosity and start a real conversation.

responses.french: Le bourbon doit être distillé à partir d'au moins cinquante et un pourcent de maïs, et vieilli en fûts de chêne neufs.
responses.english: Bourbon must be distilled from at least 51% corn and aged in new oak barrels.
responses.phonetic: luh BUR-bohn dwah ay-truh dees-tee-LAY ah par-TEER doh MWAHN sank-TAHNT ay UN poor-SAHN duh mah-EES, ay vee-YAY-ee ahn FOO duh SHEN NUF.

phrases.text_local: Je voudrais faire une dégustation de bourbon, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY fair oon day-goos-tah-SYON duh BUR-bohn, seel voo PLAY.
phrases.cultural_note: Using 'une dégustation' for bourbon — rather than just 'un tasting' — shows you're applying French sensory appreciation ('l'art de la dégustation') to an American spirit. Distillers love it.

responses.french: Avec plaisir ! On vous propose trois expressions : le single malt, le rye et notre cuvée spéciale.
responses.english: With pleasure! We offer three expressions: the single malt, the rye, and our special cuvée.
responses.phonetic: ah-VEK play-ZEER ! on voo pro-POZ TRWAH ex-press-YOHN : luh SAN-gul MOLT, luh RY ay notr koo-VAY spay-SYAL.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the barrel aging process
phrases.text_local: Combien de temps le bourbon vieillit-il en fût ?
phrases.pronunciation: komb-YAN duh TAHN luh BUR-bohn vyay-YEE-teel ahn FOO ?
phrases.cultural_note: Barrel aging ('le vieillissement en fût') is a concept deeply familiar to French wine and Cognac culture. This question signals a genuine connoisseur mindset.

responses.french: En général, entre quatre et huit ans. Mais certains bourbons vieillissent plus de vingt ans.
responses.english: Generally, between four and eight years. But some bourbons age for more than twenty years.
responses.phonetic: ahn zhay-nay-RAL, ahn-truh KAT-ruh ay WEET ahn. may sair-TAN BUR-bohn vyay-YEES ploo duh VAN ahn.

phrases.intent_en: Buy a bottle as a gift
phrases.text_local: Je cherche une bouteille à offrir en cadeau. Qu'est-ce que vous recommandez ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh shairsh oon boo-TAY ah oh-FREER ahn kah-DOH. kes-KUH voo ruh-koh-mahn-DAY ?
phrases.cultural_note: Bringing back a local spirit as a gift ('un cadeau') is a wonderful tradition. A Kentucky bourbon from a craft distillery is a genuinely impressive and personal gift for a French host.

responses.french: Pour offrir, je vous conseillerais notre édition limitée — elle est présentée dans un très beau coffret.
responses.english: For a gift, I'd recommend our limited edition — it comes in a beautiful gift box.
responses.phonetic: poor oh-FREER, zhuh voo kohn-say-yuh-RAY notr ay-dee-SYON lee-mee-TAY — el ay pray-zahn-TAY dahn zun tray BOH kof-RAY.
